name: Rapitán y Aísa by Alto del Borau
name_original: Rapitán y Aísa por Alto del Borau
region_intro: Road cycling around Jaca offers diverse terrain within the Aragonese Pyrenees. The region is characterized by majestic mountains, deep valleys, and significant mountain passes, providing varied challenges for cyclists. Routes frequently traverse through Pyrenean landscapes, including dense forests and historical sites. The area features well-paved surfaces suitable for road bikes, with options ranging from moderate rides to challenging ascents. Best road cycling routes around Jaca The most popular road cycling route is San Juan de la Peña Circular from Jaca, a 31.7 miles (51.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 38 minutes to complete. This difficult route passes historic monasteries and offers panoramic Pyrenean viewpoints. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Valle del Aragón – Puerto de Somport y Astún desde Jaca, a difficult 40.3 miles (64.8 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and includes the notable Puerto de Somport. Local road cyclists also love the Aísa Valley Loop from Jaca, a 35.4 miles (57.0 km) trail leading through picturesque Pyrenean scenery, often completed in about 2 hours 48 minutes. Road cycling around Jaca is defined by majestic Pyrenean mountains, challenging passes, and historic monasteries. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate valley rides to demanding mountain ascents. The routes in Jaca are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 4000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Jaca's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Jaca?
A: There are over 40 road cycling routes around Jaca, offering a wide range of experiences. These include 5 easy routes, 16 moderate routes, and 25 challenging routes, catering to various fitness levels.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Jaca?
A: The region around Jaca is suitable for outdoor activities year-round. For road cycling, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant temperatures and stable weather conditions. While some higher mountain passes might be affected by snow in winter, many routes remain accessible, making it a versatile destination.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes near Jaca?
A: Road cycling routes around Jaca traverse diverse terrain within the Aragonese Pyrenees. You can expect majestic mountains, deep valleys, and significant mountain passes. Routes often lead through dense forests and feature mostly well-paved surfaces, offering a mix of challenging ascents and scenic valley rides.

Q: Are there easy road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Jaca?
A: Yes, Jaca offers several easier road cycling routes. While many routes involve significant climbs, there are 5 routes specifically categorized as easy, providing a gentler introduction to road cycling in the Pyrenees.

Q: Where can I find challenging road cycling routes with significant climbs around Jaca?
A: For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, Jaca offers 25 difficult road cycling routes with substantial elevation gains. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927550350/valle-del-aragon-puerto-de-somport-y-astun-desde-jaca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valle del Aragón – Puerto de Somport y Astún desde Jaca&lt;/a&gt; feature over 1200 meters of climbing and include notable passes such as the Somport Pass, which is part of the Aragonese Way of Saint James.

Q: What scenic views or landmarks can I expect to see on road cycling routes around Jaca?
A: Road cycling routes around Jaca are rich in scenic beauty. You'll encounter breathtaking Pyrenean landscapes, including dense forests and conglomerate escarpments. Many routes offer panoramic views of the Aragon valley. Notable natural features include various mountain passes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5013399" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Somport Pass&lt;/a&gt; and stunning canyons such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1433000" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ossau Valley&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historic sites or cultural attractions accessible by road bike near Jaca?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in history. Many routes pass by significant historical sites, such as the spectacular Romanesque Monastery of San Juan de la Peña. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e923656296/san-juan-de-la-pena-circular-from-jaca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Juan de la Peña Circular from Jaca&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example of a route that combines challenging cycling with cultural exploration. You might also encounter the medieval Bridge of San Miguel or the impressive International Station of Canfranc.

Q: Are there road cycling routes around Jaca that are circular?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes around Jaca are designed as circular loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927613265/aisa-valley-loop-from-jaca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aísa Valley Loop from Jaca&lt;/a&gt; offers a picturesque ride through Pyrenean scenery, returning to your starting point.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Jaca?
A: The road cycling experience in Jaca is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars. Cyclists often praise the majestic Pyrenean mountains, the challenging passes, and the opportunity to explore historic monasteries. The diverse network of routes caters to different ability levels, from moderate valley rides to demanding mountain ascents, ensuring there's something for everyone.

Q: Are there options for road cycling routes that include the Alto del Borau?
A: Yes, for those looking to conquer specific climbs, routes like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e927603112/rapitan-y-aisa-by-alto-del-borau" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Rapitán y Aísa by Alto del Borau&lt;/a&gt; incorporate this notable ascent. This route offers a challenging ride with significant elevation gain, providing a rewarding experience for strong climbers.

Q: Can I find road cycling routes that pass by Peña Oroel?
A: Peña Oroel, a symbol of Jaca, offers panoramic views of the Aragon valley. While not all routes go directly over its summit, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20152372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Oroel Pass – Jaca Old Town and Citadel loop from Jaca&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging route that includes the Oroel Pass, allowing you to experience the area's iconic landscape and historical sites.
tips: Nice ascent, with a sustained slope around 8%. Magnificent viewpoint of Jaca.

This small mountain pass is barely three kilometers from the town of Borau, with numerous ramps above 10%. From Jaca, however, almost 12 kilometers are traveled to reach it, although the hard ramps do not appear at the port until the last two kilometers.

Aísa is one of my favorite towns in La Jacetania, especially because of the magnificent views of the Aspe it offers. When you cross its road by bike you will pass by the cafeteria of an accommodation with a large terrace that is usually always open and is also very used to receiving cyclists who are passing through.
One of the liveliest cities in the Pyrenees, with a lot of commercial, gastronomic and night life. Architecturally, its Romanesque cathedral and citadel stand out.
